name: Portland (Oregon, United States)

colors.symbolism: the forests and the green city
Portland's rivers — the Willamette and Columbia
the narrow fimbriations separating the blue and yellow elements from the green field

description: A green field bearing a central white four-pointed star (a hypocycloid) from which radiate four blue stripes — each bordered by L-shaped (squared) yellow elements — outlined by narrow white fimbriation against the green background.
history: Designed in 1969 by Portland resident and graphic designer R. Douglas Lynch, the original design (with a blue canton bearing the city seal) was adopted on 17 December 1969. City Ordinance 176874, adopted on 4 September 2002, removed the canton and established the simpler radiating-star design — the form now in use.

features.symbolism: the green field representing Portland's forests and its identity as a green city

a stylised four-pointed star (hypocycloid) at the centre, emanating four blue stripes each flanked by yellow squarred (L-shaped) elements; the white fimbriations separate the elements from the green ground
four blue stripes radiating from the central star, each bordered by yellow squarres (L-shaped corners): blue for the Willamette and Columbia rivers and yellow for agriculture and commerce
